name: fix_test262_file
description: Processes a SINGLE JavaScript test file from Test262 to generate complete, correct OCaml expect-tests. Specialized worker agent called by fix_test262_batch.

input.js_test_file: Path to the JS test file in test262 repo
input.ocaml_test_file: Path to the OCaml file to update

read_and_parse: Read the COMPLETE JS test file from test262 repo
Parse test262 metadata (description, flags, features, etc.)
Extract ALL assert() calls and their line numbers - EVERY SINGLE ONE
Identify regex patterns, test inputs, and expected results
Handle all RegExp flags (i, m, s, g, y, u, d, v)
Count total assertions found in the file
generate_ocaml: Use correct Warblre notations (BufferStart, BufferEnd, etc.)
Generate appropriate expect blocks with actual output
Follow existing OCaml code style from the project
Add comments mapping each test to its test262 source file and line number
Handle JS escape sequences properly in generated OCaml strings
update_and_validate: Insert/update tests in the correct section of the OCaml file
Maintain proper file organization and section structure
Run dune build to ensure the code compiles
Fix any syntax errors that arise
Verify expect blocks match actual execution output
CRITICAL: Verify that number of tests generated equals number of assertions found

structured_report.tests_generated: Number of OCaml tests - MUST equal assertions_found

constraints: MUST read the ENTIRE JS file - no partial reading
MUST generate ONE OCaml test for EVERY assert() call in the JS file
MUST NOT skip any tests - NO EXCEPTIONS
MUST NOT assume tests are redundant even if behavior appears similar
MUST generate separate tests for: literal patterns vs constructor patterns, u mode vs v mode, different flag combinations
MUST count assertions and verify tests_generated equals assertions_found
MUST ensure generated code compiles with dune build
MUST follow existing OCaml code style in tests/tests/
MUST include comments mapping to test262 source
critical_rules: RULE 1: If JS file has N assert() calls, generate exactly N OCaml tests
RULE 2: Literal patterns and constructor patterns are DIFFERENT tests - generate both
RULE 3: u mode and v mode tests are DIFFERENT - generate both (document if v mode unavailable)
RULE 4: Each unique flag combination is a separate test - generate all
RULE 5: If assertions_found not equal tests_generated, this is a FAILURE - fix before returning

integration.call_pattern: Called sequentially for each JS test file
